questions.q: Esther Howland, called the 'Mother of the American Valentine,' helped popularize what specific kind of Valentine product?
Pope Gelasius I is traditionally credited with designating February 14 as Saint Valentine's Day in what century?
Which English poet's 14th-century poem 'Parlement of Foules' is one of the earliest references linking Valentine's Day to romantic love?
In medieval Europe, women believed that eating which type of food on Valentine's Eve would cause them to dream of their future husband?
The oldest known Valentine's Day letter in existence was written in 1415 by a prisoner held in which fortress?
In ancient Roman tradition, the Lupercalia festival that influenced Valentine's Day customs was primarily celebrated to honor which goddess?
Victorian-era insulting and sarcastic Valentine's Day cards were composed using which substance's name as their descriptor?
In Japanese Valentine's Day tradition, what specific type of chocolate do women typically give to men they do not have romantic feelings for?
The neurotransmitter phenylethylamine, often called 'the love chemical,' is naturally produced in the brain and is also found in abundance in which common substance?
Which U.S. President's wife, Edith Kermit Carow Roosevelt, was born on Valentine's Day?
In Ovid's Metamorphoses, Cupid is depicted as falling in love with a mortal woman after being struck by his own arrows. What was her name?
The St. Valentine's Day Massacre of 1929 in Chicago resulted in the deaths of how many people in a single incident?
In Shakespeare's 'The Two Gentlemen of Verona,' which character leaves Milan to pursue a lady and become Valentine's rival?
The tradition of giving conversation hearts on Valentine's Day originated with the production of lozenges by which 19th-century company?
In medieval times, the organ believed to be the seat of love and emotion was which of the following?
Which saint, whose feast day is celebrated on February 14, was martyred under the Roman Emperor Claudius II Gothicus?
The Greek goddess Aphrodite was said to have emerged from the sea foam generated by the castration of which god?
California produces approximately what percentage of all roses grown in the United States for Valentine's Day?
In Japanese folklore, what celestial event prevents two star-crossed lovers, Orihime and Hikoboshi, from meeting except once per year?
Which writer of epistolary novels created the character Pamela, whose virtue and constancy influenced 18th-century romantic literature and Valentine's Day ideals?
The heart symbol used universally to represent love was standardized in artistic and commercial usage beginning in which century?
In South Korea, the tradition of women giving gifts to men on Valentine's Day is reversed on a different holiday called what?
Which ancient Roman holiday, celebrated in February, combined elements of purification and fertility that influenced the modern Valentine's Day customs?
In which year did the Duke of Orleans compose the oldest-known written Valentine's Day message while imprisoned in the Tower of London?
What Roman holiday's practices are directly traced as the historical origin of modern Valentine's Day celebrations?
In Roman mythology, which mortal princess did Cupid famously fall in love with, creating one of antiquity's greatest love stories?
Which Roman emperor ordered the execution of St. Valentine, an act that would cement the saint's association with martyrdom and love?
What occupational group, aside from lovers, regards St. Valentine as their patron saint?
Which Shakespeare play features the characters Valentine and Proteus as the central protagonists in a tale of friendship and romance?
What neurotransmitter is commonly referred to as the 'love hormone' and is released during emotional bonding and physical affection?
In Victorian era tradition, what was considered bad luck regarding Valentine's Day correspondence?
choices: Signing your name on the Valentine
Delivering the card on February 14th itself
Using red wax to seal the envelope
